title: Across 1,850 records, taste mismatch and repetition lead the discovery pain hierarchy.
objective: Establish the scale and ranked problem signals inside the audited sample without overstating representativeness.

layout.template: Source ledger plus ranked horizontal bars
layout.right: Top six theme counts with direct labels

method: 1,850 unique public feedback records collected from App Store, Play Store, and seven Reddit discussions.
Each record classified with a constrained schema; obvious non-discovery false positives removed conservatively.
Deterministic aggregation ranks themes across the complete 266-record discovery subset.
RAG supplies explanations and citations, never prevalence.
content.interpretation: The problem is broader than missing controls: users report a combined relevance, novelty, repetition, and correction failure.
content.limitation: Because records can carry multiple labels and the sample is self-selected, percentages rank signals within this corpus and must not be presented as Spotify-wide incidence.

visuals.alt: Source composition for 1,850 records and horizontal bars showing the six leading discovery pain counts.

speaker_notes: The closeness of ignored taste and repetition suggests a linked mechanism worth testing.
No-control ranks sixth but may be the corrective mechanism around the higher-frequency pains.
